qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Authentication bypass via AUTO_LOGIN default enables arbitrary code execution through custom components
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Identifies the same POST /api/v1/validate/code surface and validate_code() exec(code_obj, exec_globals) sink described by the advisory, though the finding frames reachability around AUTO_LOGIN defaults.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Webhook authentication disabled by default allows unauthenticated flow execution with code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: the finding concerns unauthenticated webhook-triggered flow execution and a preexisting or separately created malicious flow, not the validate endpoint exec_globals handling flaw.

selected_finding.title: Authentication bypass via AUTO_LOGIN default enables arbitrary code execution through custom components

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected qualifying finding matches the advisory-described validate_code() exec_globals root cause. Runtime evidence from the copied PoC run did not reach the live /api/v1/validate/code endpoint because Docker failed resolving the python:3.12-slim base image, but the fallback harness invoked the vulnerable AST FunctionDef compile/exec branch and showed attacker-controlled default-argument code executing through exec(code_obj, exec_globals).
evidence: Advisory GHSA-g22f-v6f7-2hrh describes unauthenticated RCE in Langflow through the exec_globals parameter handling provided to the validate endpoint.

auth_bypass_rce_via_auto_login/vulnerability_report.json lists POST /api/v1/validate/code and src/lfx/src/lfx/custom/validate.py:62-66, describing validate_code() calling exec(code_obj, exec_globals) on user-provided code.

.judge/poc_runs/auth_bypass_rce_via_auto_login/run.log shows Docker failed before server startup while resolving docker.io/library/python:3.12-slim, then the copied harness invoked the validate_code-like FunctionDef compile/exec branch.

.judge/poc_runs/auth_bypass_rce_via_auto_login/run.log and harness_validate_poc_success.txt show command output written by attacker-controlled default-argument code executed during the exec(code_obj, exec_globals) path.
blockers_to_higher_tier: The live endpoint PoC did not run because Docker could not resolve the python:3.12-slim base image from Docker Hub, so no runtime artifact proves command execution through an actual POST /api/v1/validate/code request.

poc_executions.observed_effect: Docker endpoint execution failed before vulnerable logic due to Docker Hub base-image resolution failure; the copied validate_code harness then activated the advisory root cause and created harness_validate_poc_success.txt with command output from attacker-controlled default-argument code.
